skill.name: speech-to-text
skill.description: Transcribe audio and video media to text with Whisper models through the inference.sh CLI.
skill.summary: Convert speech recordings into transcripts, timestamps, translations, and caption-ready text.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: The static Ruby backtick findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences and inline command examples. The pipe-to-shell install command is a true positive, and the skill also relies on networked CLI execution that can send audio URLs or media-derived data to inference.sh. No evidence found of prompt injection text or confirmed malicious intent, so this is high risk but not blocked.

high_findings.title: Remote Install Script Piped To Shell
high_findings.description: The quick start runs a script from https://cli.inference.sh directly through sh. This is a true positive because any compromise of the endpoint, transport path, or installation script would execute code on the user's machine before review.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command explicitly uses curl piped to sh. The surrounding install note claims checksum verification, but the shown quick start still executes remote code immediately.

medium_findings.title: Network Transcription Sends Media References To Third Party Service
medium_findings.description: The skill runs inference.sh apps with audio_url and video_url inputs. This is expected for the skill, but users may expose confidential recordings, meeting audio, or private media URLs to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented commands clearly submit media URLs to inference.sh apps. This is legitimate functionality, but it creates a privacy and data handling risk.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Is Core Functionality
medium_findings.description: The skill grants Bash access for infsh commands and documents multiple infsh app run workflows. The Ruby backtick detections are false positives from Markdown formatting, but the external command risk is real because the skill depends on a local CLI and remote app execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and command examples confirm external CLI usage. The commands appear aligned with the skill purpose and are not evidence of command injection.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation And Sample URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings are mostly documentation links, an image link, and placeholder media URLs. They are expected for a CLI guide, but they confirm that the workflow depends on external network resources.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible in user-facing documentation. They do not show exfiltration by themselves, so the severity is low.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Finding Is Not Supported
low_findings.description: The scanner reported a weak cryptographic algorithm at the description line, but the reviewed content contains no weak hash or cipher use there. The only checksum reference describes SHA-256 verification in the install note.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is a natural-language description of Whisper models, not cryptographic code. The nearby checksum reference is SHA-256, which does not support the weak-crypto finding.

dangerous_patterns.title: Pipe To Shell Installer
dangerous_patterns.description: The quick start executes a remote installer with curl piped into sh. This pattern is dangerous because fetched content is executed before the user can inspect it.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact pipe-to-shell pattern is present on the cited line. It is a known supply-chain risk even when used by legitimate tools.

dangerous_patterns.title: Networked CLI App Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the assistant to run infsh app commands against external hosted models. This can process private audio or video data outside the user's local environment.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands directly call hosted inference.sh apps with media URL input. The behavior is explicit and central to the skill.

content.value_statement: Audio and video recordings are hard to search, quote, and reuse. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through inference.sh Whisper transcription workflows.
actual_capabilities: Runs Fast Whisper Large V3 for fast transcription through inference.sh.
Runs Whisper V3 Large when higher transcription accuracy is needed.
Requests timestamped transcript segments for captions and review.
Translates supported non-English speech to English text.
Combines audio extraction and transcription for video subtitle workflows.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI, login, and network access.
Examples use remote audio_url or video_url inputs, not direct local file transcription.
Private recordings may be processed by an external inference service.
Transcript quality depends on audio clarity, language, accents, and model behavior.

use_cases.title: Meeting Transcript
use_cases.description: Convert recorded meetings into searchable text for notes, summaries, and follow-up tasks.

use_cases.title: Podcast Transcript
use_cases.description: Create a written transcript that editors can review, quote, and publish with an episode.

use_cases.title: Caption Preparation
use_cases.description: Generate timestamped transcript segments for video captions and accessibility workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Transcription
prompt_templates.scenario: A beginner has one audio URL and needs a transcript.
prompt_templates.prompt: Transcribe this audio URL with the fast Whisper model and return the full text transcript: [audio URL].

prompt_templates.title: Timestamped Transcript
prompt_templates.scenario: A user needs transcript segments for review or captions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Transcribe this recording with timestamps enabled and organize the result by spoken segment: [audio URL].

prompt_templates.title: English Translation
prompt_templates.scenario: A user has non-English speech and needs English text.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the high accuracy Whisper model to translate this speech to English and provide a clean transcript: [audio URL].

prompt_templates.title: Video Caption Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: An advanced user needs captions from a video source.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract audio from this video, transcribe it with timestamps, and prepare caption-ready transcript text: [video URL].

output_examples.input: A meeting recording URL with timestamped transcription requested.
output_examples.output: A full meeting transcript with speaker-ready paragraphs and time markers for each major segment.

output_examples.input: A French interview recording with translation requested.
output_examples.output: An English transcript that preserves the interview structure and main spoken content.

output_examples.input: A video URL for caption preparation.
output: Audio is extracted from the video source.
Speech is transcribed into timed segments.
Caption-ready text is prepared for the next video workflow.

best_practices: Use non-sensitive recordings unless the external service is approved for the data.
Request timestamps when the transcript will support captions, review, or editing.
Choose the high accuracy model for important interviews, legal review, or noisy audio.
anti_patterns: Do not run the remote installer without reviewing the source and verification process.
Do not submit confidential audio URLs without checking privacy and retention requirements.
Do not assume transcripts are perfect when audio quality, accents, or background noise are difficult.

faq.question: What models does this skill use?
faq.answer: It documents Fast Whisper Large V3 for speed and Whisper V3 Large for higher accuracy.

faq.question: Can it create timestamps?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill shows how to request timestamped segments for captions and review.

faq.question: Can it translate speech?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented translation task converts supported non-English speech to English text.

faq.question: Does it work with video?
faq.answer: Yes, but the workflow extracts audio first and then transcribes the extracted audio.

faq.question: Is it fully local?
faq.answer: No. It uses the inference.sh CLI and hosted apps, so network access is required.

faq.question: What should users check before use?
faq.answer: Users should verify installation steps and avoid sending private recordings without approval.
